title: sterculia parviflora
intro: Sterculia parviflora, also known as the small-flowered sterculia or koko tree, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Sterculia parviflora include relief from fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce blood sugar levels and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and bark are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
